Roller Types

Nylon or Steel — Which Roller Is Right for You?

We carry both nylon and steel rollers and help you choose the best option based on your door type, usage, and noise preferences.

Nylon Rollers

Our most popular upgrade. Nylon rollers with sealed ball bearings are significantly quieter than steel — the difference is immediate and dramatic. They don't require lubrication, resist rust, and last longer in Bay Area's variable climate. Ideal for attached garages or homes where noise matters.

✓ Best for quiet operation — our top recommendation

Steel Rollers

Heavy-duty steel rollers are the traditional choice — extremely durable and suitable for heavier commercial-style residential doors. They require periodic lubrication but handle high-cycle usage exceptionally well. Best for detached garages or doors that see heavy daily use.

✓ Best for heavy doors & high-cycle use
Warning Signs

Signs Your Garage Door Rollers Need Replacement

Worn rollers don't just cause noise — they put extra stress on your opener, springs, and tracks. Replacing them early prevents much more expensive repairs.

Loud Squeaking or Grinding

A squeaky or grinding garage door is almost always worn rollers. New nylon rollers eliminate the noise completely — often in under an hour.

Door Moves Unevenly or Shakes

If your door wobbles, jerks, or moves unevenly, one or more rollers are failing and causing uneven resistance on the track.

Visible Cracks or Chips on Rollers

Cracked, chipped, or flat-spotted rollers can be seen by looking at the rollers along the track — any visible damage means replacement is overdue.

Opener Straining or Running Loud

Worn rollers create extra resistance your opener has to fight. If your opener sounds labored or slower than usual, the rollers may be the cause.

Door Goes Off-Track

Worn rollers can slip out of the track entirely, causing the door to derail. Replacing rollers before they fail completely prevents this emergency.

Door Is More Than 7 Years Old

Standard rollers last 7–10 years under normal use. If your door is in this range and showing any of these signs, proactive replacement saves you from a bigger repair.

Our Process

How We Replace Your Garage Door Rollers

Roller replacement is one of our fastest services — most jobs are done in under 90 minutes with immediate results you'll feel and hear.

1

Inspect All Rollers & Track

We inspect every roller along the full track, checking for wear, cracks, rust, and misalignment before recommending the right replacement.

2

Choose the Right Roller

We recommend nylon or steel based on your door weight, usage, and noise goals — and show you both options with honest pricing before starting.

3

Replace All Rollers

We replace every roller at the same time — not just the obviously worn ones. Replacing all ensures balanced, smooth operation and prevents uneven wear.

4

Lubricate & Test

After installation we lubricate the track and hinges, then run full open/close cycles to confirm the door moves smoothly, quietly, and evenly.
